name: Mental Health Outpatient Visit Procedure Codes
description: CPT and HCPCS codes for outpatient mental health visits including psychiatric diagnostic evaluation, psychotherapy, and E/M office visits used in mental health context. Used in follow-up after hospitalization for mental illness (FUH), follow-up after ED visit for mental illness (FUM), and antidepressant medication management (AMM) measures.
source: Public CMS/clinical references — placeholder for official NCQA value sets

codes.description: Psychiatric diagnostic evaluation with medical services
Psychotherapy, 30 minutes with patient when performed with an E/M service (add-on)
Psychotherapy, 45 minutes with patient when performed with an E/M service (add-on)
Psychotherapy, 60 minutes with patient when performed with an E/M service (add-on)
Psychotherapy for crisis; each additional 30 minutes (add-on)
Family psychotherapy with patient present, 50 minutes
Group psychotherapy (other than of a multiple-family group)
Office/outpatient visit, established patient, low complexity (when used for MH follow-up)
Office/outpatient visit, established patient, moderate complexity (when used for MH follow-up)
Office/outpatient visit, established patient, high complexity (when used for MH follow-up)
